title: Windows Editions, Requirements, Lifecycle, and Migration Decisions · PBQ Checkpoint
summary: A support panel compares near-miss cases for windows editions, requirements, lifecycle, and migration decisions and accepts only safe, authorized, causal, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible decisions. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for Windows support and migration decision packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare current Windows edition capabilities for identity, management, encryption, virtualization, remote access, updates, hardware, and licensing.
Evaluate processor, architecture, memory, storage, firmware, UEFI, Secure Boot capability, TPM, graphics, display, internet, and account requirements.
Treat Windows 10 exam knowledge as a dated legacy-support and migration case after its general support end, with current exception lookup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Windows Editions
steps.instruction:
Order the Windows Editions CompTIA A+ support HTTP evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Windows Migration Choice
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Windows Migration Choice: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Unsupported Windows 10 devices and specialized apps create pressure to bypass Windows 11 eligibility without current lifecycle or recovery evidence. The quiz variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Choose upgrade, replace, isolate, extend, virtualize, retire, or redesign using support, hardware, application, data, risk, cost, access, and rollback evidence.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Windows Migration Choice.
steps.why: Retrieving Windows Migration Choice through the concrete Windows support and migration decision packet case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Windows Migration Choice, keep Windows Migration Choice connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose upgrade, replace, isolate, extend, virtualize, retire, or redesign using support, hardware, application, data, risk, cost, access, and rollback evidence.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Windows Migration Choice, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Windows Migration Choice precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Windows Migration Choice, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Unsupported Windows 10 devices and specialized apps create pressure to bypass Windows 11 eligibility without current lifecycle or recovery evidence. The quiz variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Choose upgrade, replace, isolate, extend, virtualize, retire, or redesign using support, hardware, application, data, risk, cost, access, and rollback evidence.

For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Windows Migration Choice, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Bypassing hardware checks is equivalent to obtaining supported compatibility.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Windows Editions to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Windows Editions.

checks.description: The decision applies Windows Requirements to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Windows Requirements.

checks.description: The decision applies Windows10 Boundary to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Windows10 Boundary.

checks.description: The decision applies Windows Lifecycle Lookup to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Windows Lifecycle Lookup.

checks.description: The decision applies Windows Migration Choice to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Windows Migration Choice.

checks.description: The decision applies Windows Migration Choice to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Windows Migration Choice.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using choose and upgrade plus specific case evidence.
